prompt:
Ticket: dry-run mode for the mill shift-report generator.

The generator in this crate takes the stand-controller feed plus CLI-style flags, aggregates per machine, and returns rendered output files and a run summary; a shim outside the crate drops the files into the historian folder. Ops wants to preview a run during shift handover without anything landing in the drop folder, so we are adding a --dry-run flag that has to thread through the whole pipeline: config, the pipeline driver, every writer, and the summary plugins.

Agreed behavior, signed off with ops:

1. New flag `--dry-run`, default off. Validated with the same rigor as the existing flags: it must stop tripping the unknown-flag rejection, while misspellings of it are still rejected like any other typo.
2. Live runs (flag absent) stay byte-identical to today. Same file map, same summary, zero drift.
3. A dry run renders NOTHING into the returned file map - it comes back empty.
4. A dry run's summary spells out exactly what would have happened, in this order: first the banner line `DRY RUN - no files written`; then one line per selected writer, in the order writers are registered, reading `plan: <filename> (<N> bytes, <M> lines)` where N and M are measured on the exact content the writer would have produced (M is the newline count, and the word is always 'lines', even when M is 1); then every selected summary plugin's lines, unchanged except each prefixed with `[dry-run] `.
5. Selection and validation behave exactly as live: plan lines appear only for selected writers, and unknown writer/summary names are rejected with the same errors as today.

Worked example with the standard morning fixture (stand-1 produced 120 / downtime 35, stand-2 produced 98 / downtime 52, stand-3 produced 143 / scrap 7 - an 85-byte 4-line CSV and a 178-byte 6-line day digest):

DRY RUN - no files written
plan: machine_totals.csv (85 bytes, 4 lines)
plan: shift_digest_day.txt (178 bytes, 6 lines)
[dry-run] machines: 3
[dry-run] produced total: 361
[dry-run] downtime total: 87 min
[dry-run] scrap total: 7
[dry-run] alert: stand-2 downtime 52 min

The new acceptance tests in tests/dry_run.rs pin this end to end; the existing suite in tests/report_pipeline.rs must stay green, untouched. `cargo test` is the bar and both test files are read-only.
